segments.text: Now, another Sutta we come to is 3.7.63. On a certain occasion, the resulted one was
uning about among the Goseulans, attended by a great company of monks, and he came to
Venaga, Pura, a Brahmin village of the Goseulans. Now the Brahmin housemothers of Venaga,
Pura, heard the news that go to mother recluse. Son of the Suckins, who went forth as a
wanderer from the Suckins clan, had come to Venaga Pura. Today, a fair report was
spread about Goseulans. The recluse that the exalted one is thus. It is he that Arahan, who is a
fully enlightened one, built back in knowledge and practice, well-fared, well-known, unsupposed,
charioteer of men to be tamed, teacher of devas and mankind, a Buddha and exalted one. He makes
note this world together with the world of devas, maras and Brahmas, together with the
most of recluse and Brahmin's, both of devas and mankind, having himself come to know it thoroughly
for himself. He teaches Dhamma that is lovely in the beginning, lovely in the middle, lovely in
the end, both in the letter and spirit, in all its fullness he preaches the holy life that is
utterly pure, well indeed for us if we could get the sight of Arahan such as these. Then the
Brahmin housefathers of Venaga Pura came to see the exalted one, but coming to see to him some
saluted the exalted one, and sat down at one side, some greeted the exalted one, and after the
exchange of greetings and courtesy sat down at one side, some raised the joint palms to their
foreheads in respect to the exalted one and sat down at one side, some proclaimed the name and
clan and did the same, well others without saying anything just sat down at one side, just
offer a word to comment. You see these people when they come to see the Buddha, they behave in different
ways, some draw a lot of respect for the Buddha, then they sit down to one side, some people not
so much respect and sit down to one side, some of this see the name and clan and sit down to one
side, but you notice here that all of them sit down to one side, and when they want to speak
to the Buddha, this is a kind of Indian tradition, and actually reminds me that I heard sometimes
in the West, some of our Asians, they go and see a monk like it heard, the ties would go and sit
lower than the monks, and I see that the three lanterns when they talk to a monk out of respect,
they will stand, I'll sit down, they stand, and of course to the ties, it's quite different.
So just now we sit at the three lanterns stand out of respect when they talk to a monk,
why do they stand? Because if you look into the Sutras, you find when the divas come to see the Buddha,
the divas out of respect, they stand all the divas stand up and they talk to the Buddha.
So different people have different ways of showing respect now to continue on, then as he does
said, watch a goatah, the brahmin of an Agapura said this to the exalted one. It is wonderful master
Goatama, it is marvelous master Goatama, how serene is the worthy Goatama's heir, how clear and
translucent his complexion. Just as the yellow due to be fruit in autumn is clear and translucent,
even so is the worthy Goatama's complexion. Even as a palm tree fruit just used to be from the
stock is clear and translucent, even so is the worthy Goatama's complexion. Just as a drinker
of red gold brought in a crucible by a clever goldsmith, definitely beaten and laid on a yellow
crop, shines and blazes and glitters, even so the worthy Goatama senses account, his complexion
is clear and translucent. Now master Goatama, as to those couches both high and broad, such as
the sofa, the divine or divine, together with the couplets of goats hair, of diverse colors,
white couplets, couplets of wool, decorated with flowers, quilts of cotton wool, embroidered
counterparts and rocks, heard on both sides, rocks heard on one side, couplets, gems studied and
of silk, dances, carpets, rocks for elephants, horse and chariot, rocks of antelope,
skins, floor spreads of the height of the katali deer, couches with awnings and a red
boasts to at each end, all such seats both high and low, doubtless the worthy Goatama can get
as he pleases, can get them without tall and trouble, and the Buddha said,
from it, as to all these things, indeed, we wondrous get them hardly, that is how we get them,
that if cotton, they are not to be made use of, I just talk here to say,
that here you find that the Buddha is saying that wondrous the word is paribachaka, wondrous,
renunciants, hardly get these luxurious things, couches and rocks and animal skins and all
then, and the Buddha is saying that even if they get them, they are not to be made
use of, in other words, the Buddha is not allowed his monks to use luxurious items,
some of these items during those times, the Buddha's times were considered very luxurious,
but some of the things are nowadays and might not be considered luxurious, so it depends on
the times also, and then the Buddha said, now Brahmin, there are these three couches both high
and broad, which I can get both here and now as I please, without tall and trouble, but are the three,
here the high broad couches, so let's deal, that which is sublime, and high broad couches of the
harians, these are the three couches I can get here and now as I please, without tall and
trouble, and the Brahmin asks, pretty must go to Mah, or what sort is that high broad couches,
so let's deal, which the word ego to Mah gets here and now as he pleases without tall and trouble,
and the Buddha said, in this case Brahmin, when I am living dependent on a certain village or
suburb, I get myself roped in the morning, taking bold and outer rope, I enter that village or
suburb to bang, when I return from my arms round and I am eaten my meal, I make for the age of a forest,
there I gather together whatever grasses or leaves, there are into one place and sit down cross
holding my body straight and setting my coolness in front of me, that's a lieu from sense
desires, a lieu from unprofitable states of mind, I enter on the first jhana, which is accompanied
by thought directed and sustained, born of seclusion, delightful and pleasurable, and abide therein,
I just stop for a while to make some comment, you see the Buddha, he and his disciples,
he generally go for arms round, and arms round is one of those things in the Buddha's teaching,
which is very good for a monk, because in this ritual path, we are supposed to cut off our ego,
eliminate our ego, and when we go on arms round, it is a very good practice for that purpose,
because you have to make a, you feel very humble when you go on arms round,
that's why it's not an easy thing to do, so that nowadays, not many monks also do that,
the other thing is you see that the Buddha, when he, he sees he makes up his meal,
he goes to the age of a forest, the Buddha is disciples and generally and not allowed to stay deep into
the forest, because they are not allowed to live on the fruits or whatever trees and leaves,
and all that in the forest, they are supposed to live dependent on lay people, to allow lay people,
the chance to make merit, that's why they go to lay people's houses for their food, and after that,
they go to the age of the forest, not deep into the forest to meditate, and then here you can see the Buddha,
what he does, he sees he catches the grasses and the leaves together, makes them into a
comfortable pile and then he sits on it, then to continue, then by calming down thought,
directed and sustained, I enter on the inward calm, that zingleness of mind, apart from thought,
directed and sustained, more non-concentration, delightful and pleasurable, which is the second
genre, and abide therein, then by the fading out of delight, I become equanimous, to remain
mindful and composed, and experience with the body, that happiness of which the RN say,
the equanimous mindful man lives happily, and I enter on the third genre and abide therein,
then abandoning pleasure and pain and light, by the coming to an end of the joint sorrow which I
had before, I enter and abide in the fourth genre, free of pain and free of pleasure, a state
of perfect purity of mindfulness and equanimity. Now Brahmin, when I have reached in such a state,
if I walk up and down, as such time my walking is to me celestial, if I stand, at such time,
my standing is celestial, if I sit, my sitting is to me celestial, if I lie down celestial is the high
broad couch I lie on, that Brahmin is what I mean when I speak of the high broad couch celestial,
which I get as I please without dawn and trouble, in the Brahmin's head, one who must go tomorrow,
marvelous, must go tomorrow, who but the worthy go tomorrow could get a high broad couch celestial such
as he pleases without dawn and trouble, I just stop here for a moment, here the Buddha is talking
about a four, four genres that he attains, one by one, and you find in the sutrasana, the Buddha
is very often talking about genres, genres are states of mental concentration, where the mind goes
deep into itself and deep bliss arises, so the four genres is a very high state where the
breath is supposed to stop all together, and here you can see the Buddha says that when he
comes out of the four genres, he's in such bliss that if he walks, he appears like he's walking
in heaven, if he's standing, if he's like he's standing in heaven, sit and lie down, etc. So
this only shows that when the person comes out of genres, the hindrances are still
very low, when a person that is janna, the hindrances naturally come to a very low level
that hindrances are the five, which is the first one essential desire, second one is
illogular anger, third one is sloth and top, fourth one is restlessness, restlessness and remorse,
third one is down, so because the hindrances have become so low and in fact some of the
sutras you can find the Buddha says that when a person that is janna, the hindrances are abandoned,
so here he's abandoned but not for a person that is in janna or normal person unless he's
in arahan, hindrances are abandoned but not totally rooted out, not totally eliminated,
only in the arahan, hindrances are totally rooted out, but still the here, the Buddha is saying
that when he attained the fourth janna and comes out of it, now he's in high bliss,
but the bliss of sometime can weigh over, now to continue the Brahmin asked,
but master goodama, what is that high broad couch supply, the Buddha replied, here in Brahmin
when I'm living dependent on a certain village, I said try to say try to be born,
then setting mindfulness in front of me, I abide, suffusing one quarter of the world with a heart,
possessed of goodwill, beta, likewise the second, third, and both waters, and in like manner,
above below across everywhere, broad thoughts and conditions, the whole world,
do I abide, suffusing with a heart possessed of goodwill, beta, that is widespread,
grown great and boundless, free from enmity and untrubble, and I do likewise with a heart
possessed of compassion and similarly with a heart of joy, modita possessed of equity,
that is widespread and untrubble, and trouble, is of the poor Brahmin, the others, and then the Buddha
now Brahmin, when I have reached such a condition, if I walk up and down, my walking is to
me sublime, my standing, my sitting, my lying down is to me sublime, that is what I mean,
when I speak of the sublime high broad couch, which I can get here and now is at peace without
tall and in trouble, and the Brahmin said, wonderful master goodama, marvelous master goodama,
who else but worthy goodama could be the high broad couch sublime, I stop here in the moment,
to say that this cultivation of the poor Brahmin, we haras and poor supreme abiding,
and also to do with jada, because these states also states of jada, when he can refuse the world
with these poor Brahmin, we haras which is metah, karuna, modita, and uplica, good will, compassion,
joy, and equity, to continue with the Brahmin asked, but pre master gothama, what is that
Aryan, high broad couch, and the Buddha said, here in Brahmin, when I am living dependent on some
village, etc., then setting mindfulness in front of me, I know that for certain, passion is
abandoned by me, it is cut off at the root, made like a palm tree, stop, made such as not to grow again,
unable to sprout again in the future time, hatred is abandoned by me, delusion is abandoned by me,
made unable to sprout again in the future time, that Brahmin is the Aryan couch, broad and high,
which I can get. Now Brahmin, when I have agreed, but I have reached such a condition,
if I walk up and down, my standing, my sitting, my lying down is to be Aryan,
that is what I mean when I speak of the Aryan, high broad couch, which I can get here and now,
as I please, without tomorrow and trouble, and the Brahmin said, wonderful master gothama,
marvelous master gothama, who else, but the worthy gothama could do so, excellent master gothama,
excellent master gothama, this is one reason what is overtroed or holds up a light in the darkness,
even so in diverse ways as damma been set forth by the worthy gothama, be here, do go for
refuge, the worthy gothama, do damma, do the order of monks, be the worthy gothama,
except as a leaf, follow from this leaf, or alive, last, who have so thick and refuge,
so that's the end of the sutra, so here you can see that the holy life,
these are some of the fruits of the holy life, there is deep happiness and bliss, that can be
caught from the holy life, if a person practices, if they are going not such this happiness,
who would want to practice the holy life, because when we practice the holy life, they are material
discomforts, there are some suffering, the physical sense, but because there is happiness in this
spiritual sense, that it compensates about the physical suffering that the monk undergoes,
and playing this spiritual happiness that the monk gets, or a person practices the holy path,
gets something that lasts, not like the worldly things that are so short, lived, and then the
end of the sutra, here again, you see that the person takes refuge with the Buddha, damma, and the
Bikku Sangga, and the sutra is 3.7.64, that's how I heard, most of all the sutra,
this does have I heard, that sometimes it's missed out, there is no good. On a certain occasion,
the exalted one was staying near Rajagaha, on the waltsis peak, now at that time, Sarabha,
so called the one who had not long since deserted this damma binaya, also thus speaking in the
company at Rajagaha, and he said, I understand the damma of the reclusors who are suckin sons,
it is because I understand it that I have deserted the damma binaya. I'll stop here while to
comment, what is this damma binaya? You find in the sutra that the Buddha's teachings, the Buddha
calls the damma binaya. The Buddha never calls his teachings to be darker or to be darker,
he always in the sutra, he calls it damma binaya. And damma is defined in the Angutranikaya as the sutras.
I think in the chapter of the fours, I'll put on the fours in the Angutranikaya, the Buddha says,
damma is sutras, and Vinaya is the disciplinary court for monks and nuns. So in this case,
there is this monk called Svapas. He had been a monk before under the Buddha, but he disrobe and left
the Buddha's monks and he joined the external sacachetics. So this shows that even during the Buddha's
time, even when the Buddha was alive, there were certain people who could not follow the Buddha's teachings,
even the Buddha was unable to teach such people. That's why, in the sutras, the Buddha said,
I only teach him a movement, because there are some men who are not timetable, who would allow themselves
to be tame. So even those kind of persons, the Buddha cannot change. So we have to be careful
that the Buddha cannot liberate all beings in only those that are ready to be tame.
The Buddha can't help. That's a continue. Then the number of monks having moved themselves,
taking bull and outer rope entered Raja Gaha to back, and those monks heard Sarabha, the
wanderer, speaking in the company at Raja Gaha. These words, so when they had done their
backing rounds and had returned and eaten their meal, they went to the exalted one, and on coming
to him, saluted him, they respite to him and sat down at one side, so seated, they said this to the
exalted one. Lord, Sarabha, the wanderer, has not long since deserted this Dharma vinyan. At Raja Gaha,
he is speaking thus to the company. By understand the Dharma of the reclusives who are
sucking sons, it is because I understand it that I have deserted that Dharma vinyan.
Lord, it would be well that the exalted one were to go to snake riverbank to the wondrous
park, where Sarabha the wondrous is out of compassion for him, and he exalted one consented my
silence. So the exalted one rising up from his solitary meditation in the evening,
went to snake riverbank to visit Sarabha the wondrous in the wondrous park,
reaching him, and reaching there he sat down on the seat made ready.
Having done so, the exalted one said this to Sarabha the wondrous.
Is it true I hear Sarabha that you say, I understand the Dharma of the reclusives who are
sucking sons, it is because I understand it that I have deserted that Dharma vinyan.
At these words Sarabha the wondrous was silent. Then a second time they exalted one spoke to Sarabha
the wondrous. Speak Sarabha, how is it that you have understood the Dharma of the reclusives who are
sucking sons? If your knowledge is incomplete I will complete it for you. If it is complete,
I shall receive it gladly. And the second time Sarabha the wondrous was silent.
And for the third time the exalted one spoke to Sarabha the wondrous.
Sarabha, it is by me that the Dharma of the sucking sons who are reclusives have been reviewed.
Speak Sarabha, how is it that you have understood the Dharma of the reclusives who are sucking sons?
If your knowledge is incomplete I will complete it for you. If it is complete I shall receive it gladly.
And the third time Sarabha the wondrous silent. They are upon the wondrous of Radhaka-ha,
said this to the Sarabha the wondrous. Friend, whatever you may have to ask
what the Dharma the reclusives gives you the opportunity to do so. Speak reverence Sarabha.
If your knowledge is incomplete go to the Dharma of the Dharma, the Dharma of the Dharma will
complete it for you. If it is complete he will receive it gladly.
At these words Sarabha the wondrous remains silent, confused, hanging his head looking down
at this appointed man unable to make reply. So the exalted one, seeing him in this
condition said to those wondrous. Wondrous, if anyone should say to me you have not perfect
knowledge of these things, though you claim to be fully enlightened. I should closely examine
him, question him and talk with him. He does closely examine, question and talk with,
would surely and inevitably be reduced to one or three conditions. Either he would show the
question by another and direct the talk to an alien subject or he would display anger,
melis and salkiness or he would sit silent, confused, hanging his head, looking down
words at this appointed man and able to make reply. This has now Sarabha the wondrous.
Wondrous, if anyone were to say to me the Dharma preached by you for the purpose of utterly
destroying Dukha, does not lead those who act accordingly to such a goal. I should do likewise
and he would be reduced to the same condition as Sarabha the wondrous. Then the exalted one,
having tries, uttered his lines draw in the wondrous path on the bank of Sting River,
departed through the air. Now not long after the departure of the exalted one,
those wondrous assels Sarabha the wondrous on all sides with a torrent of abuse and poking fun
at him said, friends Sarabha, even as a decrepit jackal in the great forest, thinking to utter
a lion's roar can only let out just a jackal's scream. So you rent Sarabha, thinking to utter
the lion's roar which not but go to the mother recluse can utter. You give just such a jackal's scream.
Friends Sarabha, just as a poor little hand, things to grow like a cock, and after all let's out,
let's out just a poor little hand's cackle. So you thinking to utter the cock crow that none
but go to mother recluse can utter, let out just a poor little hand's cackle. Friends Sarabha,
just as a young bull cock, when the cock and his empty, things he values deeply,
that can all bull. So to you two friends Sarabha, think to utter the deep value that none but go
to mother recluse can utter. Thus did those wondrous assels Sarabha, the wondrous with a torrent of
a few spooking fun that him. This is the end of the sutta. This is the interesting sutta because
it shows the Buddha's character, he found that his Sassana is religion was spoken bad of by
this Sarabha, so he went to defend it and he saw this Sarabha apart. And also it's interesting
after speaking to Sarabha, he departed by a flying off. This one of the revocations,
as well as the Buddha, showed his sackle power.
